summary: Lure package silently loads unrelated scoped dependency on import
details: @sql-access/nodesql@1.1.9 is a lure package. The shipped index.js is an unmodified copy of the MIT-licensed feross/buffer library, but at module top level it executes `var ins = import('@sqlite-node/createsql');` (index.js line 11). The `ins` binding is never read anywhere else — the import exists solely for its side effect of loading and executing whatever code @sqlite-node/createsql ships, in the consumer's process, the moment any caller `require()`s or imports @sql-access/nodesql. The package metadata is incoherent in ways consistent with deliberate disguise: package name suggests a SQL-access library, README headline claims `bare-stream` (`Streaming data for JavaScript`) and instructs installers to run `npm i @sql-access/nods` (a third name), repository points at `guilderguzman/sql-link`, declared license is ISC while the source file header is MIT/feross-buffer. The buffer library has no legitimate need for a SQLite-named dependency. The combination of hollow cover code (re-shipped buffer), name/README/repo mismatch, and a fire-and-forget side-effect import of an unrelated scoped sibling is the dropper-via-dependency pattern: the install of this package silently pulls and executes attacker-controlled code from a separate package on import.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
